attribution: Original brief notes by the project author with citations to Wilderness Medical Society guidelines and standard medical literature.

hypothermia.findings: Shivering present, tachycardia, vasoconstriction, alert mental status.
Shivering may be lost, decreased mentation, bradycardia, atrial dysrhythmias.
Coma, fixed pupils, ventricular dysrhythmias, asystole. Treat as potentially viable until rewarmed.

heatIllness.criteria: Core temp typically below 40 C; profuse sweating; mental status preserved; nausea, headache, weakness.
Core temp typically 40 C or higher; CNS dysfunction (confusion, seizures, coma); sweating may be absent or present.
